---
title: "Science Through Poetry"
page-layout: article
toc: false
---

The ISA Time Series: A Musical

*Written during my PhD in Svalbard as part of a science outreach event for the Longyearbyen community.*

<pre class="poem">

Once upon a time, in a land far, far away,  
In a place we all call India,  
lived a little girl that went by the name Cheshtaa.

She lived in a city of twelve million,  
all having the same vision.

When all her friends wanted to become  
doctors, engineers or lawyers,  
little Cheshtaa did not care about the dollars.

She wanted to study life  
in extreme habitats...  
or on another planet.

But there was no such study  
like that in India.

Damn it!

As you can see,  
she was exposed to marine biology.

No... she wasn't scared of boats  
or seasick—  
don't go by the chronology.

Despite her fears and her very sad survival skills,  
she moved to another continent, with only seven suitcases.

Maybe that was an overkill?!

She travelled through several countries  
on a student visa,  
discovering new places, new cultures,  
and many eateries.

She the travelled to several countries
on a student visa with multipe enteries, 
She was in places where they didnt speak what she spoke,
but nonehtless saw reindeers, yetis and famous statues despite being broke.

To find the answer to her questions,
she studied
studied  
and ahem studied, 

Until she GRADUATED!

Confused about where life would take her next, 
she decided to go for a PhD which could be complex..

At the age of 24, she was going to explore,
Organisms living in extreme habitats furthermore...

To her surprise, she had to analyse,
changes in PROTIST diversity, at the University!

Why were these little ones changing in numbers you may ask,
To find this out was infact her task!

She read and read and read and found, 
The Arctic was warming up Atlantic water all around

Could this be the reason they change in number?
From winter to winter and summer to summer?

She set sail on her boats every month with Audun, and Charlie and Edmund...

To collect and filter samples of water
And to keep in her conscience copepod slaughter
She also collected data for temperature from a CTD, 
And that of light and salinity all from the sea..


After filtering all the water for environmental DNA,
she sent off her samples to a land far far away (Canada)

In the meantime she got the opportunity to do other cool works,
I guess studying at UNIS has its own perks!


When she got the results what do you think she saw?
What I am about to tell you will make you drop your jaw!

It wasnt changing climate that was affecting these cuties,
It was more light and seasons that was changing the species.

If youre wondering, here is a figure of watermasses, 
its not just about how hot or cold it is, there are many different classes,
Dont you see the amount of warm Atlantic water we are getting through the years??
There are no trends shown in my data and that brings me to tears...

Do you see in 2014 the amount of red?? 
How organisms dont get affected by this just doesnt get in my head!

Everyone told her "Just write about seasonality.", 
No result is also a result you see!
She came up with the idea of only looking at data during the polar night, 
Surely that is affected by warm Atlantic water if theres no light?

BUT no again, cmon WHAT?!?
How can it still be seasonal despite all that?!

For 3 years she worked and worked and worked but did she find out??

You would just have to stay tuned to clear your doubt!

</pre>

---

<div style="text-align:center;">

[← Back to Outreach](outreach.qmd){.hero-button}

</div>
